bitfields.name: IFLASH_BOOTEN
bitfields.description: Enable internal flash as primary boot media

bitfields.name: IFLASH_DUAL_EN
bitfields.description: Enable dual image boot from internal flash as primary boot media

bitfields.name: EFLASH_BOOTEN
bitfields.description: Enable external flash (FlexSPI) as primary boot media

bitfields.name: EFLASH_DUAL_EN
bitfields.description: Enable dual image boot from external flash as primary boot media

bitfields.name: REC_FLEXSPI
bitfields.description: Enable external flash(FlexSPI) as recovery boot media

bitfields.name: REC_LSPI
bitfields.description: Enable external flash(LSPI) as recovery boot media

bitfields.name: REC_BOOT_EN
bitfields.description: Recovery boot enable

bitfields.name: BOOT_SPEED
bitfields.description: Boot speed. Selects the core frequency and voltage to use during ROM execution. 00b: 48MHz, FRO192, MD mode // default boot speed. If CMPA header is not valid, should boot from 48MHz MD mode. 01b: 96MHz, FRO192, SD mode 10b: 200MHz, SPLL, OD mode 11b: 250MHz, SPLL, OD mode // The actual speed will be depending on test results

bitfields.name: CMPA_HEADER
bitfields.description: CMPA Header marker should be set to 0x5963. After this header is set, all non-zero values will take effect; leaving all settings at 0xff will cause undefined behavior. It is recommended to set all values to 0x00 before setting the CMPA header value

bitfields.name: ISP_UART_EN
bitfields.description: ISP interface enable

bitfields.name: ISP_SPI_EN
bitfields.description: ISP interface enable

bitfields.name: ISP_CAN_EN
bitfields.description: ISP interface enable

bitfields.name: ISP_I2C_EN
bitfields.description: ISP interface enable

bitfields.name: ISP_USB_EN
bitfields.description: ISP interface enable

bitfields.name: ISP_PIN_ENTRY
bitfields.description: Disable ISP mode entry through pin assertion

bitfields.name: ISP_DM_ENTRY
bitfields.description: Disable ISP mode entry through debug mailbox command

bitfields.name: ISP_API_ENTRY
bitfields.description: Disable ISP mode entry through ROM API call. ISP mode can be entered through ROM API invocation

bitfields.name: ISP_FT_ENTRY
bitfields.description: Disable ISP mode entry on image authentication failure

bitfields.name: FLASH_REMAP_SIZE
bitfields.description: Flash remap size. FLASH_REMAP_SIZE defines the size of the secondary boot image (the range of flash addresses that will be remapped)in internal flash, where remapped address end = (FLASH_REMAP_SIZE + 1)

bitfields.name: EXT_CMPA_32B_SIZE
bitfields.description: Customer extended CMPA size expressed as multiple of 32 bytes. Total CMPA area = CMPA_BASE_ADDR + 32

bitfields.name: REC_BOOT_LED
bitfields.description: Assert on recovery boot. ROM drives the GPIO pin high, identified by this field whenever primary boot fails and fall through to recovery boot source. Note, set the field to 0xFF as default and when not using the feature. [4:0] GPIO Pin number. P7_31 is not supported. [7:5] GPIO port number. Ports 0 -3 are are allowed. If this feature is not use then set this field to 0xFF

bitfields.name: ISP_BOOT_LED
bitfields.description: Assert on ISP fall through. ROM drives the GPIO pin high identified by this field whenever primary boot fails and execution falls through to ISP mode. [[4:0] GPIO Pin number. P7_31 is not supported. [7:5] GPIO port number. Only 0 - 4 ports are allowed. If this feature is not use then set this field to 0xFF

bitfields.name: BOOT_FAIL_LED
bitfields.description: Assert on fatal errors during boot. ROM drives the GPIO pin high identified by this field whenever primary boot fails due to fatal errors before locking-up/reset. [4:0] GPIO Pin number. P0_0 and P0_1 are not supported. [7:5] GPIO port number. Only 0 - 4 ports are allowed. If this feature is not use then set this field to 0x00

bitfields.name: POWERDOWN_TIMEOUT_SECS
bitfields.description: Powerdown timeout: ISP mode peripheral detection timeout value in seconds. If a non-zero value is program and peripheral activity is not detected within the number of seconds specified here, then the device will go to power down mode to conserve power

bitfields.name: WDOG_TIMEOUT_COUNT
bitfields.description: WDOG timeout: Upper 16 bits of 24-bit count value in WWDT0_TC register Timeout value in seconds. The lower 8 bits of WWDT0_TC are set to 0. When a non-zero value is programmed in this field ROM configures the watch dog timer to reset the device on timeout before passing execution control to user code

bitfields.name: QSPI_AUTO_PROBE_EN
bitfields.description: Quad/Octal-SPI flash auto probe feature enable

bitfields.name: QSPI_DUMMY_CYCLES
bitfields.description: Quad/Octal-SPI dummy cycles for read command. If a non-zero value is programmed in this field, then the value is used to override the default number of dummy cycles for a fast read command read from the serial flash’s SFDP information. Note: this field is only used if FLEXSPI_AUTO_PROBE_EN is set

bitfields.name: QSPI_FREQUENCY
bitfields.description: Q/O-SPI flash interface frequency

bitfields.name: QSPI_RESET_ENABLE
bitfields.description: Use O/QSPI_RESET_PIN to reset the flash device

bitfields.name: QSPI_RESET_GPIO_PORT
bitfields.description: When FLEXSPI_RESET_ENABLE = 1, this field determines the GPIO port number to use for O/QSPI reset function

bitfields.name: QSPI_RESET_GPIO_PIN
bitfields.description: When FLEXSPI_RESET_ENABLE = 1, this field determines the GPIO pin number to use for O/QSPI reset function

bitfields.name: QSPI_HOLD_TIME
bitfields.description: Delay after reset before accessing Quad/Octal-SPI flash devices. Note, for POR in addition to this wait time FLEXSPI_PWR_HOLD_TIME is added

bitfields.name: QSPI_PWR_HOLD_TIME
bitfields.description: Delay after POR before accessing Quad/Octal-SPI flash devices in addition to delay defined by FLEXSPI_HOLD TIME field

bitfields.name: QSPI_PORT
bitfields.description: Quad SPI port

bitfields.name: QSPI_DELAY_CELL_NUM
bitfields.description: Delay cell numbers for Flash read sampling via DQS (either internal loopback or external DQS). The value provided here is loaded into the FLEXSPIn_DLLnCR

bitfields.name: QSPI_IMAGE_OFFSET
bitfields.description: Any offset in memory mapped FlexSPI Flash area could be remapped to offset zero to support eXecute In Place (XIP) of image programmed at different offset. This allows to build all update images with same RO base address, which are programmed at offset 0 or higher offset. FlLEXSPI_IMAGE_OFFSET field specifies the offset location of the second image. FLEXSPI_REMAP_IMAGE_SIZE field specifies the size multiple to determine the size of area to be remapped. If this field is left blank boot ROM will not enable FlexSPI remap feature. The physical flash offset is computed as below: physical offset = FLEXSPI_IMAGE_OFFSET

bitfields.name: QSPI_REMAP_IMAGE_SIZE
bitfields.description: Any offset in memory mapped FlexSPI Flash area could be remapped to offset zero to support eXecute In Place (XIP) of image programmed at different offset. This allows to build all update images with same RO base address, which are programmed at offset 0 or higher offset. FLEXSPI_IMAGE_OFFSET field specifies the offset location of second image. FLEXSPI_REMAP_IMAGE_SIZE field specifies the size multiple to determine the size of area to be remapped. b'0000 Remap size = FLEXSPI_IMAGE_OFFSET

bitfields.name: LSPI_SSEL_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default SPI_SSEL ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as SPI_SSEL pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: LSPI_SSEL_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by REC_SPI_SSEL_PIN

bitfields.name: LSPI_SSEL_X
bitfields.description: SPI chip select number. FlexComm SPI interfaces supports up to four chip selects

bitfields.name: LSPI_PIN_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined pins for 1-bit SPI recovery boot pins

bitfields.name: UART_RX_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default UART RX ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as UART_RX pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: UART_RX_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by UART_RX_PIN

bitfields.name: UART_ISP_FLEXCOM
bitfields.description: Identifies the FlexComm used for UART ISP mode

bitfields.name: UART_TX_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default UART TX ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as UART_TX pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: UART_TX_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by UART_TX_PIN

bitfields.name: I2C_SCL_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default I2C SCL ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as I2C_SCL pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: I2C_SCL_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by I2C_SCL_PIN

bitfields.name: I2C_ISP_FLEXCOM
bitfields.description: Identifies the FlexComm used for I2C ISP mode

bitfields.name: I2C_SDA_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default I2C SDA ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as I2C_SDA pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: I2C_SDA_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by I2C_SDA_PIN

bitfields.name: CAN_RX_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default CAN RX ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as CAN_RX pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: CAN_RX_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by CAN_RX_PIN

bitfields.name: CAN_ISP_INST
bitfields.description: Identifies the instance used for CAN ISP mode

bitfields.name: CAN_TX_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default CAN TX ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as CAN_TX pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: CAN_TX_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by CAN_TX_PIN

bitfields.name: CAN_BAUD_RATE
bitfields.description: CAN baud rate. Baud rate configured during CAN ISP mode

bitfields.name: SPI_SCK_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default SPI_SCK ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as SPI_SCK pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: SPI_SCK_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by SPI_SCK_PIN

bitfields.name: SPI_ISP_FLEXCOM
bitfields.description: Identifies the FlexComm used for SPI ISP mode

bitfields.name: SPI_MOSI_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default SPI_MOSI ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as SPI_MOSI pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: SPI_MOSI_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by SPI_MOSI_PIN

bitfields.name: SPI_MISO_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default SPI_MISO ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as SPI_MISO pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: SPI_MISO_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by SPI_MISO_PIN

bitfields.name: SPI_SSEL_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default SPI_SSEL ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as SPI_SSEL pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: SPI_SSEL_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the ALT pin mux function to use for the pin selected by SPI_SSEL_PIN

bitfields.name: ISP_SPI_SSEL_X
bitfields.description: SPI chip select number. FlexComm SPI interfaces supports up to four chip selects

bitfields.name: USB_Vendor_ID
bitfields.description: usb vendor id

bitfields.name: USB_Product_ID
bitfields.description: usb product id

bitfields.name: USBx_VBUS_PIN
bitfields.description: Override default USB0_VBUS_DETECT ISP pin. Identifies the pin to be used as USB0_VBUS detect pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: USBx_VBUS_FUNC_SLOT
bitfields.description: Identifies the pin mux function slot

bitfields.name: EXT_XTAL_CLK
bitfields.description: USB PHY input clock frequency. For USB high speed PHY external XTAL input is needed. This field specifies the frequency of XTAL used on board. • 000 - 24 MHz input clock (Divide by 15) • 001 - 30 MHz input clock (Divide by 16) • 010 - 32 MHz input clock (Divide by 20) • 011 - Reserved • 100 - 20 MHz input clock (Divide by 24) • 101 - 19.2 MHz input clock (Divide by 25) • 110 - 16 MHz input clock (Divide by 30) • 111 - 12 MHz input clock (Divide by 40)

bitfields.name: PRE_DIV
bitfields.description: Enable Pre-divider. Needed to support 48 MHz and other high speed crystals

bitfields.name: I2C_SLAVE_ADDR
bitfields.description: I2C slave address A 7-bit Address used for selecting our device on shared I2C bus system. By default ROM uses 0x10 as slave address. If this address conflicts with another slave on board customer could use this field to override the address

bitfields.name: ISP_USB_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined GPIO for USB VBUS detect function during ISP mode

values.name: CUST_DEFINED_USB0_VBUS
values.description: Customer defined GPIO for USB0_VBUS detect

values.name: CUST_DEFINED_USB1_VBUS
values.description: Customer defined GPIO for USB1_VBUS detect

bitfields.name: ISP_CAN_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined CAN ISP pins

bitfields.name: ISP_SPI_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined SPI ISP pins

bitfields.name: ISP_I2C_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined I2C ISP pins

bitfields.name: ISP_UART_CUST
bitfields.description: Use customer defined UART ISP pins

bitfields.name: NIDEN
bitfields.description: Non Secure non-invasive debug enable

bitfields.name: DBGEN
bitfields.description: Non Secure debug enable

bitfields.name: SPNIDEN
bitfields.description: Secure non-invasive debug enable

bitfields.name: SPIDEN
bitfields.description: Secure invasive debug enable

bitfields.name: ISP_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: ISP boot command enable

bitfields.name: FA_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: Controls enablement of Set_FA & Set_Brick commands

bitfields.name: ME_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: Flash Mass Erase cmd enable

bitfields.name: Inverse_value
bitfields.description: inverse value of [15:0]

bitfields.name: NIDEN
bitfields.description: Non Secure non-invasive debug Fixed state

bitfields.name: DBGEN
bitfields.description: Non Secure debug Fixed state

bitfields.name: SPNIDEN
bitfields.description: Secure non-invasive debug Fixed state

bitfields.name: SPIDEN
bitfields.description: Secure invasive debug Fixed state

bitfields.name: ISP_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: ISP command Fixed state

bitfields.name: FA_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: Controls enablement of Set_FA & Set_Brick commands are fixed state

bitfields.name: ME_CMD_EN
bitfields.description: Mass erase flash command fixed state

bitfields.name: ME_DEVCFG_DIS
bitfields.description: Disable erasing of DEVCFG page on mass erase command

bitfields.name: DBG_KEYFLUSH
bitfields.description: Flush keys in SGI keystore before enabling debug access

values.name: SGI_KEYS_CLEARED
values.description: SGI Keys are cleared before debug access is granted

bitfields.name: UUID_CHECK
bitfields.description: Enforce UUID match during Debug authentication

values.name: DEBUG_CERT_ALL_DEVICES
values.description: Debug certificate can be used with all devices

values.name: DEBUG_CERT_PER_DEVICE
values.description: Debug certificate per device with matching UUID is needed

bitfields.name: Inverse_value
bitfields.description: inverse value of [15:0]

bitfields.name: VENDOR_USAGE
bitfields.description: Upper 16 bits of vendor usage field defined in DAP. Lower 16-bits come from customer field area (CFPA.DBG_REVOKE_VU)

registers.name: Reserved 0x0004C
registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

bitfields.name: SEC_BOOT_EN
bitfields.description: Secure boot enforcement. This field defines the minimum image verification procedure (CRC32, CMAC, ECDSA sign). The Image type field in header indicates the type of verification data (checksum or signature) included in it

values.name: ONLY_CRC32_OR_CRYPTO_SIG
values.description: Only CRC32 or cryptographically signed (CMAC or PKI - ECDSA/ MLDSA) images are allowed

values.name: ONLY_CRYPTO_SIGNED
values.description: Only cryptographically Signed (CMAC or PKI - ECDSA/ MLDSA) images are allowed

values.name: ONLY_PKI_SIGNED
values.description: Only PKI signed (ECDSA or MLDSA) images are allowed

bitfields.name: LP_SEC_BOOT
bitfields.description: Secure boot option for low-power wake from power-down & deep-powerdown. For CFPA/CMPA do CRC check always

values.name: CRC_CFPA_CMPA_AND_IMAGE
values.description: CRC check for CFPA/CMPA & CRC32 check of active image

values.name: CRC_AND_JUMP_TO_VECTOR
values.description: CRC check for CFPA/CMPA & jump to vector address specified in CFPA

values.name: CRC_AND_CMAC_IMAGE
values.description: CRC check for CFPA/CMPA & CMAC check of active image

bitfields.name: DICE_CSR_KEY_TYPE
bitfields.description: Define the DICE csr and key generation type

bitfields.name: ENF_CNSA
bitfields.description: Enforce CNSA suite approved algorithms for secure boot, secure update and debug authentication

values.name: USE_PERFORMANCE_CRYPTO
values.description: Use performance crypt algos (ECC P-256, SHA256 & AES 128)

values.name: USE_CNSA1_CLASSIC_CRYPTO
values.description: Use CNSA 1.0 specified classic crypto algorithms. (ECC P-384, SHA384 & AES256)

values.name: USE_CNSA2_PQC_CRYPTO
values.description: Use CNSA 2.0 specified PQC crypto algorithms.( MLDSA-87, ML-KEM, SHA384 & AES256)

values.name: USE_HYBRID_PQC_CRYPTO
values.description: Use hybrid (PQC + classic) crypto algorithms.(ECDSA P-384 + MLDSA-87, ML-KEM, SHA384 & AES256)

bitfields.name: ENF_TZM_PRESET
bitfields.description: TrustZone-M mode

values.name: ENFORCE_PRESET_TZM_DATA_1
values.description: Enforce preset TZM data in image manifest

values.name: ENFORCE_PRESET_TZM_DATA_2
values.description: Enforce preset TZM data in image manifest

values.name: ENFORCE_PRESET_TZM_DATA_3
values.description: Enforce preset TZM data in image manifest

bitfields.name: FAST_BOOT_EN
bitfields.description: Fast boot enabled. First boot after update local-CMAC using DUK-Auth key is created and used for sub-sequent boot authentication

bitfields.name: ACTIVE_IMG_PROT
bitfields.description: Protection of active image. This field defines protection of flash area occupied by the active image. Only applicable to internal flash

values.name: PROTECT_CFPA_FLASH_ACL
values.description: Protection is defined using the CFPA FLASH_ACL settings

values.name: WP_ACT_IMG_STICKY_GLBAC2
values.description: Write protect active image area with sticky lock. GLBAC2 is used. FLASH_ACL settings are ignored

values.name: WP_ACT_IMG_NOSTKY_GLBAC4
values.description: Write protect active image area without sticky lock. GLBAC4 is used. FLASH_ACL settings are ignored

values.name: XOM_ACT_IMG_STICKY_GLBAC6
values.description: XOM protect active image area with sticky lock. GLBAC6 is used. FLASH_ACL settings are ignored

bitfields.name: FIPS_SHA_STEN
bitfields.description: Enable self-test for SHA2 block on power-up. Needed for FIPS certification. If this field is non-zero run self-test and log result in SYSCON->ELS_AS_BOOT_LOG1[FIPS]

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY
values.description: On failure enter ISP mode for recovery

values.name: ON_FAIL_LOCK_DEVICE
values.description: On failure lock the device to enforce power-cycle

bitfields.name: FIPS_AES_STEN
bitfields.description: Enable self-test for AES block on power-up. Needed for FIPS certification. If this field is non-zero run self-test and log result in SYSCON->ELS_AS_BOOT_LOG1[FIPS]

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY
values.description: On failure enter ISP mode for recovery

values.name: ON_FAIL_LOCK_DEVICE
values.description: On failure lock the device to enforce power-cycle

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY

bitfields.description: Enable self-test for CMAC block on power-up. Needed for FIPS certification. If this field is non-zero run self-test and log result in SYSCON->ELS_AS_BOOT_LOG1[FIPS]

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY

bitfields.name: FIPS_KDF_STEN
bitfields.description: Enable self-test for KDF block on power-up. Needed for FIPS certification. If this field is non-zero run self-test and log result in SYSCON->ELS_AS_BOOT_LOG1[FIPS]

values.name: ON_FAIL_ISP_RECOVERY

bitfields.name: DIS_NXP_FW
bitfields.description: Block NXP signed SB3 loading

values.name: ALLOW_OEM_NXP_SIGNED_SB3
values.description: Allow only OEM + NXP signed SB3 FW

bitfields.name: SKIP_DICE
bitfields.description: Skip DICE computation

bitfields.name: DICE_INC_NXP_CFG
bitfields.description: Include NXP area (IFR1) containing specific part configuration data defined during chip manufacturing process in DICE computation

bitfields.name: DICE_INC_CUST_CFG
bitfields.description: Include data from CMPA page in DICE computation

bitfields.name: DICE_INC_NXP_FIELD_CFG
bitfields.description: Include NXP field programmed area (NFPA) containing in-field ROM patch in DICE computation

registers.name: SBL_START_ADDR
registers.description: Secondary boot loader (SBL) or First Mutable Code (FMC) image start address. When FMC_SBL_EN is set ROM checks for presence of valid image at this location. If not present then goes to recover boot path

registers.name: ERR_LOG_ADDR
registers.description: start address of error log buffer in on-chip ram

registers.name: ROTKH0
registers.description: Root of Trust Key Hash is SHA256 or SHA384 of RoTK public. Hash algorithm is selected based on RoTK EC type (secp256r1 -> SHA256 or secp384r1 -> SHA384). Same RoTKs and RoTKTH values are shared between debug authentication, SB3.1 firmware updates container and signed boot image based on CMPA.RoTKx_Usage

registers.name: CUST_MK_SK_KEY_BLOB0
registers.description: CUST_MK_SK is stored in form of RFC3394 blob and it is used by bootloader to decrypt SB3.1 encryption key during processing of SB file by bootloader. CUST_MK_SK is generated during device provisioning process by HSM_KEY_GEN (random key) or by HSM_STORE_KEY (user defined key) commands. To store this key into CMPA, SB_STORE_KEY command should be used

registers.name: PQC_ROTKH0
registers.description: Root of Trust Key Hash is SHA256 or SHA384 of RoTK public. Hash algorithm is selected based on RoTK EC type (secp256r1 -> SHA256 or secp384r1 -> SHA384). Same RoTKs and RoTKTH values are shared between debug authentication, SB3.1 firmware updates container and signed boot image based on CMPA.RoTKx_Usage

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_0
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins high after reset. Each bit corresponds to the pin in GPIO port 0. When set ROM drives the corresponding pin high as soon as possible. By default most pins come-up as tri-stated inputs. This feature allows customer to specify active drive pins soon after reset instead of waiting till complete boot. Note, if a pin is selected in both QUICK_SET_GPIO_x and QUICK_CLR_GPIO_x fields then pin will be set to high-level with quick transition to low

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_0
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field. Note, if a pin is selected in both QUICK_SET_GPIO_x and QUICK_CLR_GPIO_x fields then pin will be set to high-level with quick transition to low

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_1
registers.description: Drive GPIO 1 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_1
registers.description: Drive GPIO 1 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_2
registers.description: Drive GPIO 2 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_2
registers.description: Drive GPIO 2 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_3
registers.description: Drive GPIO 3 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_3
registers.description: Drive GPIO 3 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_4
registers.description: Drive GPIO 4 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_4
registers.description: Drive GPIO 4 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_5
registers.description: Drive GPIO 5 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_5
registers.description: Drive GPIO 5 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: Reserved 0x00120
registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

registers.name: Reserved 0x00124
registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

bitfields.name: GCM_MODE
bitfields.description: GCM mode enable

values.name: REGION_ENABLED_CTR_MODE
values.description: Region is enabled in CTR mode

values.name: REGION_ENABLED_GCM_MODE
values.description: Region is enabled in GCM mode

bitfields.name: AHBERR_DIS
bitfields.description: Disable AHB bus error. If GCM authentication fails generates bus error or not

bitfields.name: IPED_CTX0_START_ADDR
bitfields.description: Upper 24-bits of IPED region 0 start address. Lower 8 address bits are always 0

bitfields.name: LOCK_EN
bitfields.description: Lock the IPED context settings

bitfields.name: IPED_CTX0_END_ADDR
bitfields.description: Upper 24-bits of IPED region 0 end address. Lower 8 address bits are always 0

bitfields.name: DICE_ECDSA_CERT_BUF_SIZE
bitfields.description: dice ecdsa cert buf size

bitfields.name: DICE_MLDSA_CERT_BUF_SIZE
bitfields.description: dice mldsa cert buf size

registers.name: DICE_x509_MLDSA_SRAM_ADDR
registers.description: If set as zero, then use the 0x30002000 RAM address as the default

registers.name: DICE_ALIAS_KEY_SRAM_ADDR
registers.description: If set as zero, then use the 0x30019000 RAM address as the default

registers.name: MLDSA_CERT_TEMP_ADDR
registers.description: Certificate template structure for MLDSA alias key identity

registers.name: MLDSA_CERT_TEMP_HASH0
registers.description: Root of Trust Key Hash is SHA256 or SHA384 of RoTK public. Hash algorithm is selected based on RoTK EC type (secp256r1 -> SHA256 or secp384r1 -> SHA384). Same RoTKs and RoTKTH values are shared between debug authentication, SB3.1 firmware updates container and signed boot image based on CMPA.RoTKx_Usage
